essay_text:
I was born on a blustery morning in January of 2002 in Arlington, Texas. I was a very big baby weighing in
at 11 pounds 11 ounces and being 18 inches long, but I was an even bigger strain on my parents. Because
of my timing, my mother could not work due to her pregnancy and my dad had been laid off yet again from
his job. My family of six - my mother, father, two sisters, brother, and me - were living inside of a two-
bedroom apartment. When my mother got out of the hospital, my parents realized they could not afford to
pay rent so we moved. Another apartment, but this time in a less-safe part of Arlington. This would be
where I grew up until the second semester of second grade. Inside of this apartment complex marijuana
and underage drinking seemed like a joke compared to the rape and gang violence that occurred.
Gambling or "shooting dice" was also commonplace. There were no real boundaries for these people as
they would often gamble on the front steps of the bible study that was being held just 10 feet in the same
direction.

Living inside these conditions, and my mother being a counselor to drug-addled, problematic young
people, incentivised her to give my brother and me the opportunity for a better life than the people around
us. My mother would accomplish these goals of raising us to become successful by enrolling us in the
local Bible Study ensuring that we were somewhere safe after school. We were not allowed outside past
8pm at first as that is when it got dark outside, but this curfew would soon be pushed back to 7pm as the
Arlington Police Department implemented their own 8pm curfew within the apartment complex. My mother
would then enroll my brother and I inside of the school's local chess club to ensure that we were being
productive with our time. She would continue this push of clubs and activities desperately trying to
distract us from the crime and violence going on around my brother and I. When we finally moved away
from Arlington in 2010 to Crowley, just south of Fort Worth, she kept the same high standard out of both
fear and pride that we would not live up to our full potential. The expectations she formed out of fear,
hope, and mystical motherly intuition would morph me into the person I am today. Her dedication to see
me rise above my situation and grow into the stellar student I am today would drive me to do the best |
could in everything I did. This would cause me to bend over backwards to achieve the grades that I knew
were possible, breaking a little along the way. I would come to school sick multiple times as I knew that I
could not risk the damage it might do to my grades and overall knowledge. I would stay up until 5:30am,
sleep for 30 minutes. and wake up at 6am to go to band rehearsal while doing more homework during
lunch and in between classes. It is this unwavering dedication to excellence that has made me who I am
today.
prompt: Some students have a background, identity, interest, or talent that is so meaningful they believe their application would be incomplete without it. If this sounds like you, then please share your story.
persona_prompt: You are a writer who crafts direct, chronological narratives, focusing on significant life experiences and their transformative impact. Employ a clear, descriptive style, anchoring your story with specific, often stark details and a strong sense of cause-and-effect. Frame personal struggles as catalysts for growth, highlighting unwavering dedication and resilience, while acknowledging the emotional cost. Maintain an appreciative yet analytical tone towards influential figures in your life.
sentence_style: Predominantly moderate to long, declarative sentences, often linked with conjunctions to establish clear causal relationships. The rhythm is steady and explanatory, with occasional shorter sentences for direct impact. No sentence fragments are used.
vocabulary_level: Moderate — utilizes accessible language but incorporates precise verbs and slightly more formal nouns ("incentivised," "mystical motherly intuition," "unwavering dedication") to convey depth and analytical insight without being verbose.
structural_patterns: A clear chronological narrative, segmented by significant life stages and changes in environment. Employs strong cause-and-effect relationships, particularly linking environmental challenges and parental actions to personal development. Uses contrast between challenging origins and aspirational outcomes.
distinctive_moves: Presents stark, realistic descriptive details of difficult environments (e.g., "rape and gang violence," "gambling on the front steps of the bible study").
Directly attributes personal growth and drive to parental influence and early adversity.
Quantifies personal struggle and dedication with specific metrics and examples (e.g., "stay up until 5:30am, sleep for 30 minutes").
Concludes with a definitive statement about identity being formed by unwavering dedication and past experiences.
themes_and_preoccupations: Overcoming socio-economic hardship and dangerous environments.
The transformative power of parental dedication and high expectations.
The pursuit of excellence and the personal sacrifices it entails.
Resilience and the shaping of identity through adversity.
self_presentation: Presents as resilient, determined, and deeply appreciative of their mother's efforts. Acknowledges vulnerability and personal cost ("breaking a little") but frames it within a larger narrative of success and unwavering commitment. Confident in their achieved identity.
cultural_or_contextual_markers: Experience of growing up in a low-income, high-crime urban area (Arlington, Texas), the specific context of family struggles, and the cultural emphasis on 'a better life' driven by parental sacrifice.
avoid_in_imitation: Overly academic or esoteric vocabulary, or overly informal slang.
Excessive use of metaphor, similes, or highly figurative language.
Sarcasm, cynicism, or any tone that deviates from earnest directness.
sample_sentence: Having witnessed firsthand the quiet sacrifices my father made, I understood that true success was less about the destination and more about the relentless, often unseen, effort along the way.
